query: No... chose k to represent a number of sessions... we're up over 250 I think retained in our current observation set... it doesn't show that many because more than half probably are not officially associated with a feature_cycle, (early we didn't have a context_cycle(start) defined, so it was best guess attribution.  Our database is pretty large 190MB currently... likely don't want it growing too much larger for 1 repository... Thats my starting point... but my question is just how small SHOULD we make it.  Now that we retain the cycle_review summaries, we can be more aggressive, but want to think about the potentential W3-1 option.  After 409, my focus will probably be cleanup for a bit, rather than immediately start on W3-1
